reviews.comment:
I recently used this company for a battery replacement on my old 12.9-inch iPad Pro 3rd gen and had a really good experience. From the beginning, communication was excellent and the whole process was smooth. They first made sure of the exact iPad model before quoting.

I couldn’t visit them personally, so they arranged courier collection and delivery as part of the service, which made things very convenient. I was kept updated throughout the entire process — from courier collection, delivery to their workshop, assessment, repair progress, and even any challenges they encountered along the way.

The battery replacement itself was done very neatly, with no noticeable damage or marks from opening the screen. Overall, I’m very impressed with the service and professionalism, and I’ll definitely make use of them again in future.

reply.comment:
Hi Dawie,

We appreciate you taking the time to write this.

The 12.9-inch iPad Pro 3rd gen is one of the trickier devices to open neatly. The adhesive and the proximity of the digitiser cables make it unforgiving work, so it's good to hear the screen came back without any tell-tale marks.

Confirming the exact iPad model before quoting is non-negotiable for us. The 12.9-inch line ran through five generations that look almost identical from the outside, but the batteries, screens, and pricing all differ.

Although couriers can sometimes be slightly delayed,this is the type of service all clients expect especially when they're unable to bring their devices in personally. We have done our best to ensure that you are kept in the loop at every stage during the repair process. 

If you have any technology related questions please reach out at any time!

All the best

ZA Support IT Specialist Team

reviews.comment: I recently had an accident and dropped my M4 MacBook Air on its corner. Because my laptop is my entire workspace for my design and media projects, I was terrified about potential internal damage or losing my files. I took it to ZA Support for an impact assessment. They did a thorough internal inspection and gave me a very detailed report explaining that while the external chassis took a hit, the internal components and logic board were completely fine. They were incredibly professional, and I felt 100% comfortable trusting them with my sensitive data. Highly recommend their workshop!

reply.comment:
Hi Lamasabatha,

Thank you for the detailed feedback.

A drop on the corner of an M4 MacBook Air is the impact pattern that hides damage. Due to the aluminium unibody dissipates a lot of the energy, which is reassuring, but it also means the visible dent is rarely the full story. The only way to know for certain is to open the machine, inspect the logic board, hinge mounts, antenna lines, and battery cells, then trace any stress paths back to the impact point.

Generally Macs are primary work devices. The approach to ensure there's zero internal damage is spot on.

We're glad it was a clean outcome on the internals. 

If you have any technology questions going forward please don't hesitate to reach out.

ZA Support Team 

Practice IT. Perfected.

reviews.comment: So grateful for the world-class service from Mary and the ZA Support crew. My M4 MacBook Pro was in my laptop bag, and I had an Energade bottle on the outside of my bag. During the flight the bottle must have opened. The laptop of was covered in Energade, I took it to a few 'Apple' stores, they just told me to replace the machine. I got hold of ZA Support and from the very first interaction it was outstanding. I got a booking within 30-minutes and they fixed my Mac board. Saved me at least 60 grand in buying a new one. If you want excellent service from a company who really knows Mac, I strongly recommend you try them. I'll be recommending them to all Mac colleagues.
